mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% triggers a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units triggers a reduction in consumer spending on housing-related goods and services, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 114 indicates a supply-driven economic expansion that increases corporate profits and stock prices, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 114 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment below 137,000 units triggers a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 137,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 11650 triggers a demand-driven economic expansion that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11650 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the Consumer Price Index above 210.5 triggers a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 210.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real disposable personal income above 385750 triggers a demand-driven economic expansion that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 385750 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
